Author: Ariel Livshits
Title: Git Brain: Finally, Git That Makes Sense in VS Code & Cursor
Text:
Git Brain: Finally, Git That Makes Sense in VS Code & Cursor
I love VS Code
I love Cursor
I love AI writing most of my code
But I still hated resolving merge conflicts
And every time I typed:
git stash
git pull --rebase
git checkout feature-x
I felt like I was working around my tools.
Not with them.
In 2026, AI agents can refactor entire codebases in seconds.
But switching branches with uncommitted changes still feels risky.
That gap bothered me more than it should have.
So I built Git Brain.
The VS Code Era Is Here — But Git Is Still Stuck
Most developers today live in:
- Cursor
- VS Code
- Windsurf
- Antigravity
- VSCodium
- Trae
The ecosystem evolved fast.
AI-native workflows.
Smarter autocompletion.
Agents handling pull requests.
But Git?
Still terminal-first.
Still marker-based conflicts.
Still fragmented panels.
Still too much mental overhead.
Git never felt like it belonged inside the modern editor experience.
It felt bolted on.
Git Brain treats Git as a first-class workflow — not a side panel.
What Git Brain Actually Changes
Git Brain isn’t about “more Git features.”
It’s about reducing friction, It reorganizes Git inside your editor around three surfaces:
Activity, History and Branches
Everything else becomes secondary.
The Moment That Matters: Merge Conflicts
Let’s be honest.
You don’t judge your Git UX when everything is clean.
You judge it when things break.
When you pull and see:
<<<<<<< HEAD
=======
>>>>>>> branch
That’s not an experience.
That’s raw plumbing.
Git Brain introduces a real 3-way merge editor — Local, Result, Remote — side by side.
You navigate conflicts with shortcuts.
You apply left, right, or both.
You edit the result pane directly.
Scrolling is synced.
Conflicts are marked in the scrollbar.
When you’re done? One click: Complete Merge.
Or even better: Complete Merge & Push.
Conflicts stop being chaotic text blocks.
They become structured decisions.
If you’re coming from JetBrains, it feels natural.
If you’re coming from terminal Git, it feels like a relief.
The Silent Killer: Dirty Worktrees
Here’s the everyday scenario.
You’re halfway through something.
You want to check another branch.
Git says no.
Now what?
Stash manually?
Commit WIP?
Abort your flow?
This is where most friction happens.
Git Brain introduces Smart Pull and Smart Checkout.
You click.
It auto-stashes.
Performs the operation.
Restores your changes.
Guides you through conflicts if they appear.
No mental gymnastics.
No remembering command combinations.
Just flow.
Git Should Feel Designed
History shouldn’t feel secondary.
With Git Brain, your commit graph becomes interactive and explorable.
You scroll through history naturally.
You open a commit.
You inspect changed files.
You jump from inline blame directly into the commit in the log.
Blame a line → understand why → move on.
No context switch.
Branches stop being a dumb dropdown.
You can merge, rebase, rename, delete, compare, pull, fetch, push — all from one coherent UI.
In monorepos and multi-root workspaces, this isn’t convenience.
It’s sanity.
Stash and Blame — Finally Visible
Stash isn’t a hidden stack of commands anymore.
You create a stash from selected files.
You browse stashes visually.
You inspect their contents.
You apply, pop, drop — with conflict-aware warnings.
Blame isn’t noise either.
You get gutter annotations.
Heatmap mode.
Compact mode.
Hover metadata.
Jump straight into the Git Log.
Git stops being invisible infrastructure.
It becomes understandable.
Why This Actually Matters in 2026
AI is reshaping development.
But Git is still the backbone.
If your AI tools feel polished,
but your Git workflow feels fragile —
You’re introducing friction at the most critical layer of your work.
Git Brain aligns Git with the modern editor ecosystem:
VS Code
Cursor
Windsurf
Antigravity
VSCodium
Trae
It makes Git feel intentional again.
Who This Is For
Git Brain is for developers who:
- Moved from JetBrains and miss real Git UX
- Resolve conflicts weekly
- Work in monorepos
- Use Cursor and expect Git to match AI polish
- Are tired of typing
git stash
before switching branches
It’s not about adding more Git.
It’s about removing friction from the Git you already use every day.
Master Your Repository. Free Your Brain.
That line isn’t marketing fluff.
It’s the real goal.
When Git stops interrupting you,
you think about code again.
Try Git Brain
🌐 Website - https://git-brain.com
🧩 VS Code Marketplace
https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=git-brain.git-brain
🔓 Open VSX (VSCodium & compatible builds)
https://open-vsx.org/extension/git-brain/git-brain
If Git in VS Code or Cursor has ever felt “good enough” but not great —
Git Brain is the upgrade.
Not louder.
Smarter.
Finally, Git that makes sense.
